source_of_truth: draft.md contains manuscript prose; this registry is an audit and navigation layer.

objects.description: Simultaneous impact-only observed residual model u_t = B0 epsilon_t + eta_t with unit-variance structural shocks and additive residual noise; VAR lags and dynamic responses are outside the first paper.
For the robust transformed-cumulant route, additive residual noise is independent of structural shocks and Gaussian, so every linear transform of the noise has zero cumulants above order two.
Structural shocks are normalized to unit variance, and robust-DW candidate impacts are restricted to a normalized nonsingular space to fix scale.
Population sign-restricted set built from a covariance factor P_* P_*' = B0 B0' + V and restrictions R(P_* Q) >= 0.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: If V is nonzero, the standard sign-restricted population set is based on B0 B0' + V and generally differs from the no-noise economic sign set.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: B0(D^2 - I)B0' = V, equivalently D^2 - I = B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'}, showing why positive column rescaling generally cannot absorb additive residual noise.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: M27 common-chart reported standard-DW set: sign-admissible normalized candidates whose standardized recovered shocks pass a J-test stack containing the no-noise covariance moment and finite higher-moment independence restrictions.
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md; manuscript/derivations/dw-robust-comparison-diagnostic.md

objects.description: M25 working result: under residual noise, standard DW recovered shocks are not structural shocks. A rich covariance-whitened J-test inversion is generically empty in population, except for structural-coordinate rescaling cases or finite-moment aliases; finite samples can produce falsely small least-rejected regions around pseudo-candidates.
objects.proof_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs audit and early Monte Carlo triage

objects.description: Population and sample J-test inversion for standard covariance-whitened DW candidates: g_infty(Q)'Wg_infty(Q) and T ghat(Q)'What ghat(Q), with asymptotic rejection when the population criterion is bounded away from zero.
objects.verification_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs early MC and audit before draft theorem wording

objects.description: Robust DW-style sign-restricted candidate set that searches over a normalized impact space and uses mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u, rather than no-noise covariance factorization; M27 defines the reported J-test inversion and keeps second moments as nuisance inputs only inside fourth cumulants.
objects.verification_status: M24 conditional audit passed for a local normalized Gaussian-noise route; M27 formalized the reported set and diagnostic comparison; M28 population/repeated-seed validation passed; the larger M29 finite-sample pass supports the high-noise divergence story under primary chi-square cutoffs and shows truth-bootstrap widening as a calibration-cost audit

objects.description: Moment-equation representation of the robust DW stack: third mixed central moments and fourth mixed cumulants of z(B)=B^{-1}u, with second moments entering only as covariance products inside fourth cumulants.
objects.verification_status: M24 algebra review passed for centered third moments and fourth cumulants with covariance-product subtractions; M28 population verification passed; larger M29 chi-square-primary finite-sample pass completed, with repeated-sample, oracle, and truth-bootstrap cutoffs retained as audits

objects.description: Working result: under additive Gaussian residual noise, mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u are not shifted by noise, while second moments are contaminated; in the bivariate normalized system, local identification follows when each structural shock has a nonzero third or fourth cumulant.
objects.proof_status: conditional-local-rank-audit-passed; M28 grid validation passed; larger M29 chi-square-primary simulation pass completed for draft evidence

objects.description: M27 diagnostic statement: standard-DW accepted mass outside the robust-DW set warns that no-noise covariance-target precision may be driven by residual-noise misspecification, while robust accepted mass outside standard DW mainly records the information deliberately lost by dropping second moments.
objects.proof_status: M27 formalization complete; larger M29 chi-square-primary simulation pass completed for draft evidence

objects.description: M27 overlap metric bundle for the common normalized chart: accepted shares, symmetric Jaccard overlap, directional containment q_{S|R}=mu(S cap R)/mu(S), and warning share d_{S not subset R}=1-q_{S|R}.
Intuitive sign-set geometry is now covered by the top row of the selected M0020 residual-noise grid, which shows sign/covariance accepted sets moving as residual noise increases.
objects.output_source: Selected visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png. A separate covariance-ellipse panel is optional, not required for the first draft.

objects.description: Selected residual-noise story figure showing sign/covariance, standard-DW, and robust-DW accepted B-plane sets across increasing Gaussian residual noise using pointwise 10 percent finite-sample J-test cutoffs. The high-noise stress column has standard DW rejecting true B0 while robust DW contains it.
objects.output_source: Selected visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.md, manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png. Earlier exploratory support: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_figure.py and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_comparison.png. M28 first validation passed; M29 expanded pass supports the high-noise truth-rejection story under the same chi-square cutoffs applied researchers would use; repeated/bootstrap cutoffs are audits.

objects.description: Selected grid-pair story comparing sign/covariance, standard DW, and robust DW accepted B-plane sets. Both grids invert pointwise 10 percent finite-sample J tests in all rows. The residual-noise grid shows standard-DW truth rejection and robust-DW truth inclusion; the non-Gaussianity grid shows robust-DW widening as higher moments weaken.
objects.output_source: Selected visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.md, manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png; manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_nongaussianity_grid_figure.py, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_nongaussianity_grid_figure.md, manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_nongaussianity_grid.png. Both grid figures were corrected in M0020 to invert pointwise 10 percent J tests in all rows while keeping the robust rows free of second-moment restrictions. M28 validated truth inclusion, boundary sensitivity, repeated seeds, and weak-moment widening at the first gate; the larger M29 pass supports the high-noise divergence and weak-moment widening readings under primary chi-square cutoffs.

objects.description: Monte Carlo summary of true-B coverage-style inclusion, set width, empty-set frequency, standard-DW versus robust-DW overlap, and directional divergence warnings using the M27 metric bundle. The larger M29 finite-sample pass uses chi-square, no-noise repeated, oracle scenario truth, and truth-point residual-bootstrap cutoffs. User decision U0026 makes the chi-square rows the primary applied benchmark because these are the standard critical values a researcher would use under the no-noise DW null. The audit cutoffs quantify finite-sample size and calibration cost. The larger run uses 240 calibration replications, 120 evaluation replications, 40 truth-bootstrap replications per evaluation sample, and a 41-by-41 grid.
objects.output_source: M29 larger-pass output: manuscript/simulations/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.py, manuscript/simulations/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.md, manuscript/simulations/output/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.json. Under primary chi-square cutoffs, high-noise standard DW includes true B0 in 0.325 of evaluation samples while robust DW includes it in 0.908. Earlier M35 exploratory support: manuscript/simulations/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_triage.py, manuscript/simulations/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_triage.md, manuscript/simulations/output/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_summary.json.

objects.description: M24 adversarial review of the robust DW derivation. Conditional pass: cumulant-to-moment algebra, second-moment exclusion, normalization caveat, local rank, Gaussian-noise condition, and bias language are recorded; M28 later supplied the first population-grid validation gate.

Adversarial review of simulation design and interpretation for noisy sign-set bias, standard DW false sharpening, and robust DW comparison. M30 completed the first audit layer for the M35 triage script: conditional pass for exploratory use only; population-grid and final Monte Carlo audits remain.

First M28 validation gate for the M0020 grid pair. Exact population moment diagnostics support robust-DW truth zeros under Gaussian residual noise, standard-DW divergence under noisy covariance targets, and weak/Gaussian higher-moment widening. Repeated finite-sample seeds support the high-noise visual story at pointwise chi-square cutoffs, while leaving calibrated coverage evidence to M29.

open_audits.question: Does the M25 standard-DW J-test derivation survive audit, especially the rich-stack generic emptying claim, structural-rescaling exception, and finite-moment alias caveats?
